---
title: "LA County Wildfire Risk"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(duckdb)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(jsonlite)
library(mapgl)
library(htmlwidgets)

urls <- paste0(
  "https://data.source.coop/carbonplan/carbonplan-ocr/output/fire-risk/",
  "vector/production/v1.1.0/geoparquet/buildings.parquet/",
  "state_fips=06/county_fips=037/data_", 0:12, ".parquet"
)

urls_sql <- paste0("[", paste0("'", urls, "'", collapse = ", "), "]")

con <- duckdb::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), dbdir = ":memory:")
DBI::dbExecute(con, "INSTALL httpfs; LOAD httpfs;")
DBI::dbExecute(con, "INSTALL spatial; LOAD spatial;")

# Discover column names from schema
schema_df   <- DBI::dbGetQuery(con, sprintf("DESCRIBE SELECT * FROM read_parquet(%s)", urls_sql))
all_cols    <- schema_df$column_name
start_idx   <- which(all_cols == "rps_scott")
end_idx     <- which(all_cols == "bp_2047_riley")
target_cols <- all_cols[start_idx:end_idx]

# Read centroid lon/lat + risk columns — sample 100k rows to keep HTML size manageable
attr_sql <- paste(target_cols, collapse = ", ")
df <- DBI::dbGetQuery(con, sprintf(
  "SELECT ST_X(ST_Centroid(geometry)) AS lon,
          ST_Y(ST_Centroid(geometry)) AS lat,
          %s
   FROM read_parquet(%s)
   USING SAMPLE 100000 ROWS",
  attr_sql, urls_sql
))

DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

# Build point sf from lon/lat columns
buildings_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(df, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)

# ── USDA burn probability from remote COG ───────────────────────────────────
# Credentials from credentials.json (gitignored — never committed).
# Key names match the Source Cooperative credentials JSON format.
creds <- jsonlite::fromJSON("credentials.json")
terra::setGDALconfig("AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID",     creds$credentials$aws_access_key_id)
terra::setGDALconfig("AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY", creds$credentials$aws_secret_access_key)
terra::setGDALconfig("AWS_SESSION_TOKEN",     creds$credentials$aws_session_token)
terra::setGDALconfig("AWS_REGION",            creds$credentials$region_name)
terra::setGDALconfig("AWS_VIRTUAL_HOSTING",   "FALSE")

cog_path <- "/vsis3/us-west-2.opendata.source.coop/espm-288/espm-288-testing/BP_CA_cog.tif"
cog <- terra::rast(cog_path)

# terra::extract does not reproject automatically — match CRS first
pts_vect <- terra::project(terra::vect(buildings_sf), terra::crs(cog))

# extract() returns data frame: col 1 = row ID, col 2 = raster value
usda_vals <- terra::extract(cog, pts_vect)
buildings_sf$usda_bp <- usda_vals[[2]]

# Compute composite risk score: USDA burn probability × CarbonPlan conditional RPS
buildings_sf$rps_usda <- buildings_sf$usda_bp * buildings_sf$crps_scott

# Put rps_usda first so it's the default display; usda_bp last
target_cols <- c("rps_usda", target_cols, "usda_bp")
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Compute per-column min/max for the JS color scale
buildings_pts <- buildings_sf
stats_df <- buildings_pts |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  summarise(across(all_of(target_cols), list(
    min = ~min(., na.rm = TRUE),
    max = ~max(., na.rm = TRUE)
  )))

col_stats <- setNames(
  lapply(target_cols, function(col) list(
    min = stats_df[[paste0(col, "_min")]],
    max = stats_df[[paste0(col, "_max")]]
  )),
  target_cols
)
col_stats_json <- toJSON(col_stats, auto_unbox = TRUE)
cols_json      <- toJSON(target_cols)

# Custom 10-stop white→red scale for rps_usda (breaks as decimal fractions of %)
rps_usda_breaks <- c(0, 0.0001, 0.0002, 0.00035, 0.0006, 0.001, 0.002, 0.005, 0.01, 0.03)
rps_usda_colors <- c("#fff0f0", "#ffd6d6", "#ffadad", "#ff8585", "#ff5c5c",
                     "#ff3333", "#e60000", "#cc0000", "#990000", "#660000")
rps_usda_breaks_json <- toJSON(rps_usda_breaks)
rps_usda_colors_json <- toJSON(rps_usda_colors)

m <- maplibre(
  style  = carto_style("dark-matter"),
  center = c(-118.25, 34.05),
  zoom   = 9
) |>
  add_circle_layer(
    id     = "buildings",
    source = buildings_pts,
    circle_color = interpolate(
      column = "rps_usda",
      values = rps_usda_breaks,
      stops  = rps_usda_colors
    ),
    circle_radius       = 3,
    circle_opacity      = 0.7,
    circle_stroke_width = 0
  )

js_code <- sprintf('
function(el, x) {
  var map = this.getMap();
  var cols          = %s;
  var stats         = %s;
  var rpsUsdaBreaks = %s;
  var rpsUsdaColors = %s;

  var wrapper = document.createElement("div");
  wrapper.style.cssText = [
    "position:absolute", "top:10px", "left:50px", "z-index:1000",
    "background:rgba(30,30,30,0.85)", "padding:8px 12px", "border-radius:4px",
    "box-shadow:0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.5)", "font-family:sans-serif",
    "font-size:13px", "color:#fff"
  ].join(";");

  var label = document.createElement("label");
  label.textContent = "Risk metric: ";

  var select = document.createElement("select");
  select.style.cssText = "margin-left:6px;background:#333;color:#fff;border:1px solid #555;border-radius:3px;padding:2px 4px";
  cols.forEach(function(col) {
    var opt = document.createElement("option");
    opt.value = col;
    opt.textContent = col;
    select.appendChild(opt);
  });

  function updateLayer(col) {
    var paintExpr;
    if (col.startsWith("rps_")) {
      var stops = ["interpolate", ["linear"], ["get", col]];
      for (var i = 0; i < rpsUsdaBreaks.length; i++) {
        stops.push(rpsUsdaBreaks[i]);
        stops.push(rpsUsdaColors[i]);
      }
      paintExpr = stops;
    } else {
      var s = stats[col];
      var mid = (s.min + s.max) / 2;
      paintExpr = ["interpolate", ["linear"], ["get", col],
        s.min, "#1a9850",
        mid,   "#ffffbf",
        s.max, "#d73027"
      ];
    }
    map.setPaintProperty("buildings", "circle-color", paintExpr);
  }

  select.addEventListener("change", function() { updateLayer(select.value); });

  wrapper.appendChild(label);
  wrapper.appendChild(select);
  el.appendChild(wrapper);

  // Click popup: show all risk fields for the clicked building
  map.on("click", "buildings", function(e) {
    if (!e.features || !e.features.length) return;
    var props = e.features[0].properties;

    var rows = cols.map(function(col) {
      var val = props[col];
      var display = (val !== null && val !== undefined) ? parseFloat(val).toFixed(6) : "N/A";
      return "<tr>" +
        "<td style=\\"padding:2px 10px 2px 0;font-weight:bold;white-space:nowrap\\">" + col + "</td>" +
        "<td style=\\"padding:2px 0;text-align:right\\">" + display + "</td>" +
        "</tr>";
    }).join("");

    var html = "<div style=\\"font-family:sans-serif;font-size:12px;max-height:280px;overflow-y:auto\\">" +
      "<table style=\\"border-collapse:collapse\\">" + rows + "</table></div>";

    new maplibregl.Popup({ maxWidth: "280px" })
      .setLngLat(e.lngLat)
      .setHTML(html)
      .addTo(map);
  });

  map.on("mouseenter", "buildings", function() {
    map.getCanvas().style.cursor = "pointer";
  });
  map.on("mouseleave", "buildings", function() {
    map.getCanvas().style.cursor = "";
  });
}
', cols_json, col_stats_json, rps_usda_breaks_json, rps_usda_colors_json)

final_map <- m |> onRender(js_code)
final_map
```

---

## About This Map

Each dot represents a sampled LA County building, colored by its **Risk to Potential Structures (RPS)** — the annualized expected loss from wildfire. This map compares RPS values produced by different burn probability methodologies: even though the underlying building stock is identical, the three independent fire behavior modeling approaches yield meaningfully different risk scores, revealing how much methodology choice shapes community-level risk estimates.

| Field(s) | Source | Methodology (2 sentences) |
|:---|:---|:---|
| `rps_scott`, `crps_scott` | **Scott et al. 2024** — USDA Forest Service Rocky Mountain Research Station & Pyrologix LLC | Used the FSim geospatial fire simulator with LANDFIRE 2020 fuel data at 270 m to model burn probability, then ran FlamMap across the full seasonal weather spectrum at 30 m resolution to model fire intensity. These inputs were combined to produce conditional risk to potential structures (cRPS) for every land parcel in the contiguous U.S. |
| `rps_riley`, `bp_scott_riley`, `bp_2047_riley` | **Riley et al. 2025** — USDA Forest Service | Generated annual burn probability rasters at 270 m resolution for both present-day (~2011) and projected mid-century (~2047) climate conditions using large-ensemble fire simulations. Applying these probabilities to structure exposure values yields RPS estimates that can be compared directly to Scott et al. under current and future climate scenarios. |
| `usda_bp` | **USDA Wildfire Risk to Communities** — Dillon et al. 2024, USDA Forest Service | Produced a nationally consistent burn probability layer at 270 m using FSim with LANDFIRE 2020 fuels, then upsampled to 30 m to align with structure-level assessments. This layer is designed for standardized cross-regional comparison of wildfire likelihood across all U.S. lands. |
| `rps_usda` | **Composite (this analysis)** | Multiplies the USDA burn probability (`usda_bp`) by CarbonPlan's conditional risk score (`crps_scott`), combining a nationally standardized ignition likelihood with a structure-level conditional exposure metric. This field is not a published product — it illustrates how substituting a different burn probability source into the same RPS framework shifts the resulting risk rankings. |

**CarbonPlan Open California Risk (OCR)** is the platform that spatially joined all datasets above to individual building footprints from Overture Maps Foundation, enabling per-building comparisons across fire risk methodologies at scale.
